error.signature: AI tells a foreign visitor to China that they can pay with Visa/Mastercard everywhere, or that WeChat Pay/Alipay setup is straightforward
error.regex: (china.*payment|wechat pay.*foreigner|alipay.*foreigner|china.*credit card|china.*cashless|微信支付|支付宝|china.*tour pay|tour card alipay)

verdict.summary: China is overwhelmingly cashless via WeChat Pay (微信支付) and Alipay (支付宝); Visa/Mastercard rarely accepted outside top-tier hotels and airports. Since 2023 the PBOC opened tourist-friendly setup: foreign Visa/Mastercard can fund Alipay (with 200 USD limit per single transaction, 5000 USD/year), WeChat Pay (with foreign-card-tied bind, similar caps). Cash is increasingly refused even for taxi fares; some shops have no working POS terminal.

dead_ends.action: Plan a China trip with the assumption that 'I'll just use Visa and pay cash as backup'
dead_ends.why_fails: Many small shops, food stalls, taxi drivers, and rural vendors have NO POS terminal — payment by Visa is impossible. Cash is increasingly refused; vendors say 没有零钱 (no change) and refuse the bill. The People's Bank of China has explicitly required vendors to accept cash but enforcement is weak. WeChat/Alipay is genuinely default.

dead_ends.action: Try to register WeChat Pay or Alipay only after arriving in China
dead_ends.why_fails: Both apps require SMS verification — a Chinese phone number works best, but foreign numbers can register since 2023. The first transaction often triggers identity verification (passport + selfie), which works inconsistently from inside China. Setting up before arrival is much smoother.

dead_ends.action: Top up a Chinese-resident-style WeChat Pay balance with foreign Visa, expecting unlimited spend
dead_ends.why_fails: Foreign-card spend is capped: 200 USD per transaction, 5,000 USD per year per app. Larger purchases (laptops, designer goods) require resident-status payment. This is a 2023 PBOC tourist initiative cap.

workarounds.action: Pre-trip: install WeChat + Alipay. Verify identity with passport. Bind a Visa or Mastercard. Carry small RMB cash (~500 RMB) for backup. Register both apps — some merchants take only one.

For >5,000 USD/year spend: open a Chinese bank account (requires multi-day stay + visa + tax paperwork) OR use UnionPay-issued cards from your home country (BoC America, ICBC USA branches) which are treated as 'domestic' and uncapped.
workarounds.tradeoff: Bank account opening for short-term visitors is largely impractical; UnionPay cards have annual fees.

workarounds.action: Major hotels (Shangri-La, Hyatt), airport shops, and metro stations accept Visa. Use them as cash-out points if WeChat/Alipay fails. Some metros (Shanghai, Beijing) accept foreign contactless cards directly.
